Details: Full-scale studies with RO membranes reported removals ranging between 69 [2733] and 85 percent [2722]. Bench and pilot-scale studies demonstrated that the small molecular size of this contaminant is an important consideration when selecting a particular membrane for treatment. A bench-scale study using flat sheet RO membranes found only 2 percent removal [2054], while a pilot study with spiral wound desalination type RO membranes found up to 96 percent removal [2110]. A bench-scale study with NF membranes achieved a maximum of 48 percent removal [2089].
